rulings.Case Title: JANET BRIDGES VS. COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO 'A GOVERMENT ENTITY' et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending Appellate Review Of Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 25, 2011, Line 1, Defendant City and County of San Francisco Motion For Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending Appellate Review Of Summary Judgment - Denied. The appeal of the grant of Parker Hannifin¿s motion for summary judgment does not automatically stay any portion of this case other than the City¿s claims against Parker Hannifin. The court declines to exercise its discretion to stay any other claims. A stay would significantly delay resolution of this case, which has already been pending over two years. The City has not shown that it is likely to be successful on its appeal. Even if the City is successful on its appeal, the resolution of plaintiff¿s claims against the City prior to the finalization of the appeal might eliminate any need for the City¿s claims against Parker Hannifin and, at the very least, would provide significant guidance as to the scope of those claims. Moreover, per CCP 437c(n)(2) and (3), the granting of summary judgment in favor of Parker Hannifin does not necessarily have any affect on any of the remaining claims by or against the City. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS FIGUEROA VS. WASHINGTON MUTUAL BANK, F.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 25, 2011, Line 9, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendants JPMorgan Chase Bank and California Reconveyance Company¿s Demurrer to 1st Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, eighth, eleventh and twelfth causes of action. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the seventh, ninth and tenth causes of action to provide plaintiff an opportunity to correct the deficiencies in those claims. The second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, eighth and twelfth causes of action all relate to alleged wrongdoing that occurred at the origination of the loan, yet neither of the demurring parties are alleged to have been involved in the loan origination and Chase is not liable for any loan origination misdeeds under the purchase and assumption agreement. The eleventh cause of action fails because there is no fidcuciary relationship requiring defendants to provide an accounting. The seventh cause of action for quiet title is now insufficient because of the absence of any allegation of tender of the amount due under the loan documents. The ninth cause of action for declaratory relief is based on conclusions without any facts supporting the relief sought. The tenth cause of action is now insufficient because of the absence of any allegation of an offer to restore the consideration received under the loan documents. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STRAUSS & SHAVELLE INC. VS. STEVEN DAY, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MORTALITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moton And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 25, 2011, Line 18, PLAINTIFF STRAUSS & SHAVELLE INC.'s Notice Of Moton And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is Granted. Plaintiff has shown that it is likely to succeed on its claim for breach of contract due to defendant¿s failure to return to plaintiff all of the documents that defendant was obligated to return under the parties¿ agreements. Plaintiff has also shown that it would suffer harm if the injunction is not granted. Defendant has not shown that he would be harmed in a material way by the injunction. Defendant is required to return only documents that he received while he worked as an employee or independent contractor of plaintiff, not any documents that he has obtained since he ceased working with plaintiff, including any documents he has received from his clients or obtained as a result of his own efforts. Plaintiff must post a bond of $25,000. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)
